summary: Matched `ftCo_8009BF3C` in the common fighter cargo-throw code by replacing a large inline PowerPC assembly block with C. The change also adjusted the helper `inlineA0` so its generated code matched: direct repeated field accesses were used instead of temporary stick variables, and motion-state offset additions were split into assignment from `fp->x2CC->x4_motion_state` followed by `+=` constants. The file report showed `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoThrow.c` at 100% with `ftCo_8009BF3C` scoring 0/6000 after the change.
what_changed: Removed the `ASM bool ftCo_8009BF3C(ftCo_GObj* gobj)` implementation guarded by `MUST_MATCH`/`WIP` and replaced it with a normal C function.

Implemented `ftCo_8009BF3C` as: compute `FtMotionId msid = inlineA0(gobj->user_data)`, compare it against `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)->motion_id`, call `ftCo_8009C02C(gobj, msid)` if the motion state changed, then return `true`; otherwise return `false`.

Updated `inlineA0` to derive cargo throw motion IDs from current input: A/B input gate via `fp->input.x668 & HSD_PAD_AB`, horizontal stick threshold via `ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) >= p_ftCommonData->x98`, facing-direction test for forward/backward selection, and vertical stick thresholds via `attackhi3_stick_threshold_y` and `xB0`.
Changed several expressions from `fp->x2CC->x4_motion_state + constant` into two statements, first assigning `fp->x2CC->x4_motion_state` to `msid`, then applying `msid += 10/11/12/13`.
Removed local `float lstick_x` and `float lstick_y` temporaries from `inlineA0`, using `fp->input.lstick.x` and `.y` directly instead.
The PR report indicated the whole file and all listed functions in `ftCo_CargoThrow.c`, including `ftCo_8009BF3C`, were at 100% after this match.
smart_moves: Factored the decision tree into `inlineA0`, keeping `ftCo_8009BF3C` small and close to the assembly behavior: compute candidate motion ID, compare to current motion ID, transition only if changed.
Used `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` for the comparison while passing `gobj->user_data` into the inline helper, matching the apparent codegen needs without changing the high-level behavior.

Split base motion-state load and constant addition into separate statements; this is a common decomp matching tactic when `base + immediate` expression shape does not produce the desired instruction ordering/register allocation.
Avoided extra float locals in the stick checks; direct struct-field access can help the compiler preserve the original load/comparison order for matching.
Preserved the existing cargo throw motion offset scheme: base motion state plus offsets 10, 11, 12, and 13 for directional throw variants.
decomp_lessons: For small dispatcher functions, matching may require tuning both the function body and any inline helper it depends on; here `ftCo_8009BF3C` matched only after reshaping `inlineA0`.

When an assembly block uses a candidate motion ID initialized from the current motion ID and conditionally replaced, model that explicitly with a local `FtMotionId msid` initialized in the helper and compare it against the current `motion_id`.
Expression spelling matters for codegen: `msid = base; msid += constant;` can produce different output from `msid = base + constant;` even though the logic is equivalent.
Removing source-level temporaries can be beneficial when the target assembly reloads or uses original struct fields in a specific sequence.

Input threshold logic in fighter code often maps analog stick direction and common-data thresholds directly to motion-state offsets; preserve the original comparison boundaries (`>=`, `<=`) exactly when matching.
naming_conventions: The matched function kept the placeholder-style symbol name `ftCo_8009BF3C`, consistent with unresolved or address-based ftCommon functions.
The helper name `inlineA0` remained an internal/static inline matching aid rather than being renamed to a semantic name in this PR.
Cargo throw callbacks use `ftCo_CargoThrow{Air,F,B,Hi,Lw}_{Anim,IASA,Phys,Coll}` naming in the same file report.
Types and accessors used include `ftCo_GObj`, `ftCo_Fighter`, `Fighter`, `FtMotionId`, and `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`.
Stick and common-data fields were referenced with existing project names such as `fp->input.lstick.x`, `fp->input.lstick.y`, `p_ftCommonData->x98`, `attackhi3_stick_threshold_y`, and `xB0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed a `nofralloc` inline assembly implementation after achieving C equivalence for `ftCo_8009BF3C`.
Used an inline helper to reproduce the assembly's candidate-motion-ID calculation before the function-level compare and branch.
Separated load of `fp->x2CC->x4_motion_state` from immediate additions (`+= 10`, `+= 11`, `+= 12`, `+= 13`) to match instruction sequencing.
Used direct field accesses instead of temporary `lstick_x`/`lstick_y` locals to affect float load/register behavior.
Kept the control flow as a simple early transition path returning `true` and a fall-through returning `false`, matching the assembly's boolean return structure.
The report showed `ftCo_8009BF3C` reached 100% with score `0` of max `6000`, and the file reached 100% overall.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or document `ftCo_8009BF3C` or `inlineA0`.
Search for other fighter input-to-motion functions where `base motion state; += offset` was required for matching.
Look for PRs touching `ftCo_8009C02C` to understand the transition semantics used by `ftCo_8009BF3C`.
Search cargo throw functions using offsets 10, 11, 12, and 13 from `x4_motion_state` for naming or enum opportunities.
